target.title: fix #94040: [Bug]: nodes approve failed: GatewayClientRequestError: unknown requestId
target.body:
## Status

- **status:** 👀 ready for maintainer look

## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw nodes approve <requestId>` surfaced a raw `GatewayClientRequestError: unknown requestId` when the request id was stale or no longer pending, leaving operators without a clear recovery path during companion/node onboarding.
- Solution: When `node.pair.approve` returns the specific unknown requestId error, the nodes CLI now fetches current pending node pairing requests and prints a user-actionable message.
- What changed: `nodes approve` now reports the stale request id, shows currently pending request ids when visible, and points users to `openclaw nodes pending`. This update also keeps the existing regression test type-safe and fixes the lint failure from the first PR head.
- What did not change: gateway pairing semantics, requestId lifetime, pairing storage, auth scopes, node approval policy, device pairing CLI behavior, provider routing, session state, and channel delivery are unchanged.

## Why it matters / User impact

- **Why it matters / User impact:** This issue blocks onboarding: the companion app tells the user to run an approval command, but by the time they run it the request id can be stale or no longer pending. The previous error looked like a defect and gave no next step. The new message explains the stale request id and shows the current pending request ids, so the operator can rerun the correct approval command or inspect pending requests.

## Scope boundary

- **What did NOT change:** This patch only changes the `openclaw nodes approve <requestId>` CLI rendering for the specific gateway `INVALID_REQUEST` / stale requestId response. It does not change gateway `node.pair.approve`, node pairing persistence, request id expiration rules, node/device auth, pairing scopes, companion app behavior, Kubernetes port-forwarding, provider routing, session state, channel delivery, or `openclaw devices approve`.
- **Failure-mode boundary:** Only the exact `GatewayClientRequestError` with `gatewayCode === "INVALID_REQUEST"` and message containing `unknown requestId` is converted into the actionable CLI message. Other approval errors still flow through the existing `nodes approve failed: ...` path.

## Source-of-truth boundary

- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary is the nodes CLI command layer in `src/cli/nodes-cli/register.pairing.ts`. The gateway protocol result is valid; the CLI was the layer leaking raw protocol wording to users instead of rendering the canonical operator recovery path. This keeps compatibility with the existing gateway contract because it performs only a read-only `node.pair.list` lookup after the stale approval response and does not alter request/response shape, defaults, schema, migration, or wire behavior.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- Related open PR: #94055 (`fix(cli): improve error message for unknown requestId in devices approve`).
- Why this PR is not the same patch: the issue report shows `openclaw nodes approve 57511973-c922-444a-b5a6-d689fc6ea364`, but #94055's visible CLI change targets `src/cli/devices-cli.runtime.ts` (`openclaw devices approve`) and does not update the nodes approval command path. Its diff also contains unrelated Matrix changes, which broadens review scope beyond this issue.
- Why this PR is safer to review: this patch touches only the nodes pairing CLI command and its matching CLI coverage test. It does not alter gateway methods, Matrix code, device approval behavior, or pairing storage.
- Non-duplication claim: this PR fixes the reported command surface (`nodes approve`) with a local real CLI proof, rather than improving a sibling `devices approve` error path.

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — independent root-cause PR for #94040. #94055 is an external competing open PR for the same issue, not a merged origin/follow-up anchor.

Closes #94040

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001 (`status: ⏳ waiting on author`): this update supplies the author-side correction and fresh verification; no label was manually changed outside the contributor flow.
- RF-002 / RF-003 (the recovery hint pointed at an unregistered nested pending command): fixed the stale-request recovery hint in `src/cli/nodes-cli/register.pairing.ts` to point to the registered `openclaw nodes pending` command, and updated the regression expectation.
- RF-004 (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/nodes-cli.coverage.test.ts --reporter=verbose`): ran exactly this command after the fix; 1 file passed, 20 tests passed.
- RF-005 (`git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`): ran after the fix; it completed with no output.

## Patch quality notes

- **Patch quality notes:** Diff size is small: one production CLI file and one matching CLI coverage test file. The new catch block is intentionally narrow: it only matches `GatewayClientRequestError` with `gatewayCode === "INVALID_REQUEST"` and stale requestId wording, then performs a read-only pending-list lookup for the recovery message. It is not a broad guard or fallback masking gateway failures; unrelated approval errors are rethrown through the existing command wrapper. The output avoids raw `GatewayClientRequestError` and avoids adding an extra `Error:` prefix for the user-facing stale request message.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw nodes approve <stale requestId>` should explain that the request id is unknown and show current pending request ids instead of surfacing raw gateway protocol text.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux task worktree at committed PR head `d5fa10201c60a9fd94a1e426d6137b085855004b`, with `origin/main` fetched at `2ff1dfdebece970cb0fa579d2fc51e64c0bdc10c`, Node `v22.22.0`, and pnpm dependencies installed from the lockfile. The proof drives the real source CLI entrypoint against a local WebSocket gateway stub implementing `connect`, `node.pair.list`, and `node.pair.approve`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94452-evidence/context.env"; (cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && pnpm exec oxlint src/cli/nodes-cli/register.pairing.ts)
```

```bash
source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94452-evidence/context.env"; (cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && pnpm tsgo:core:test)
```

```bash
source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94452-evidence/context.env"; (cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/nodes-cli.coverage.test.ts --reporter=verbose)
```

```bash
source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94452-evidence/context.env"; (cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node "$EVIDENCE_DIR/nodes-approve-unknown-request-proof.mjs") > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/nodes-approve-unknown-request-proof.output.json"
```

```bash
source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-94452-evidence/context.env"; git -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

- **Evidence after fix:** The real CLI exits with code 1 but now prints the stale request id, current pending request id, and the recovery command. The gateway request trace confirms the CLI ran through `node.pair.list`, attempted `node.pair.approve` with the stale id, then listed pending requests again to build the message.

```json
{
  "scenario": "nodes approve stale requestId against gateway with current pending request",
  "cli": {
    "exitCode": 1,
    "stdout": "",
    "stderr": "nodes approve failed: Unknown node pairing requestId: stale-request\nPending requestIds: current-request\nRun openclaw nodes pending to inspect current requests."
  }
}
```

```text
connect -> node.pair.list -> connect -> node.pair.approve { requestId: "stale-request" } -> connect -> node.pair.list
```

The full nodes CLI coverage test passed:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  20 passed (20)
```

The targeted lint, core test typecheck, and `diff --check origin/main...HEAD` also passed after this update.

- **Observed result after fix:** Users no longer see the raw `GatewayClientRequestError: unknown requestId` for this stale nodes approval path. They see the stale request id, current pending request ids when available, and the command to inspect pending node pairing requests.
- **What was not tested:** No live Windows Companion App, Kubernetes port-forwarded gateway, or production gateway was used. The proof covers the local CLI-to-gateway WebSocket path for `nodes approve` and the stale/unknown requestId error branch.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix for the nodes CLI error translation boundary.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/cli/nodes-cli.coverage.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** `nodes approve stale-request` receives `GatewayClientRequestError` with `gatewayCode: "INVALID_REQUEST"` and `unknown requestId`; the CLI then lists pending requests and prints `Unknown node pairing requestId`, `Pending requestIds`, and `openclaw nodes pending` without raw `GatewayClientRequestError` text.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The test uses one-shot gateway mocks so it does not pollute the shared CLI coverage mock state, and it verifies the negative output boundary (`GatewayClientRequestError` and `nodes approve failed: Error:` are absent).
- The full `src/cli/nodes-cli.coverage.test.ts` file was run with the CLI Vitest shard to ensure adjacent nodes CLI coverage still passes.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** CLI onboarding behavior, node pairing, linked external PR #94055, and external Windows Companion/Kubernetes live-proof boundary.
- **Risk explanation:** The changed code runs an extra read-only pending-list lookup after one specific approval failure. A too-broad match could hide unrelated approval failures.
- **Why acceptable:** The matcher requires both `GatewayClientRequestError`, `INVALID_REQUEST`, and `unknown requestId`; unrelated errors are rethrown unchanged. The proof shows the exact WebSocket command sequence and the test checks that raw protocol text is removed only for this path.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The source of the user-visible failure was the nodes CLI rendering boundary: it passed a stale requestId protocol failure through as raw gateway text instead of translating that valid gateway response into the operator state needed for companion/node pairing recovery.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix is applied at the CLI rendering boundary where the unhelpful message was produced, and it uses the canonical pending-pairing list to provide the correct next action without changing the gateway contract or masking unrelated approval failures.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the targeted CLI behavior; live Windows Companion and Kubernetes port-forward reproduction are not included and are disclosed above.

target.title: fix(memory): respect QMD timeout for memory_search
target.body:
## Summary
- use the resolved QMD `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` as the `memory_search` tool deadline for QMD-backed searches
- keep the existing 15s default for non-QMD memory search paths
- strengthen the regression test so a configured 45s QMD search is still pending and un-aborted at 15s, then times out and aborts at 45s
- document that QMD `limits.timeoutMs` also controls the `memory_search` tool deadline and add a manual smoke test for checking gateway/backend logs after raising the timeout

## Manual smoke test
After applying this branch to an installation with QMD memory enabled:
1. Set `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` above 15000, e.g. `45000`, and restart the gateway.
2. Run `memory_search` from a tool-enabled agent session with a query known to take longer than 15s on that machine.
3. Inspect gateway/backend logs for that search window. The old `memory_search timed out after 15s` failure should not appear. If the search still exceeds the configured budget, the unavailable payload should report the configured timeout, e.g. `memory_search timed out after 45s`.

## Tests
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/memory-qmd.md docs/reference/memory-config.md`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `node scripts/test-extension.mjs memory-core extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts`
- `node scripts/test-extension.mjs memory-core`

target.title: Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent
target.body:
## Summary

Isolates QMD mcporter configuration per agent while preserving user/workspace-owned mcporter definitions that must stay external. For agents without an explicit user-owned mcporter server definition, OpenClaw generates a per-agent stdio config under `$STATE/agents/<agentId>/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json` with manager-scoped QMD env vars.

Supersedes #79745. This replacement PR keeps the QMD/mcporter fix and drops the unrelated Skill Workshop i18n commit that accidentally widened the superseding branch.

## Linked context

Related #79745.

## Behavior addressed

- Remote QMD servers with auth material (`headers`, env, URL credentials, refresh metadata, token-like query params) stay external and use the original mcporter config.
- Servers with `lifecycle`, daemon logging, relative `cwd`, or relative `path` are treated as user-owned and kept external.
- Bare stdio QMD servers are rewritten into a generated per-agent config under OpenClaw state.
- `startDaemon=false` omits the keep-alive lifecycle in the generated config so mcporter does not auto-launch the daemon.
- Snake-case remote endpoint keys (`base_url`, `server_url`) are recognized alongside camelCase.
- Project-scoped mcporter configs are probed before legacy home fallback.
- Discovery mode uses a clean `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` so OpenClaw state dirs do not shadow user/project configs.
- External-mode calls set `MCPORTER_CONFIG` to the discovered source path so the original config is reused.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: QMD memory search through mcporter must keep each agent on its own mcporter config/daemon state while preserving configured user/workspace mcporter servers that contain auth, lifecycle, logging, cwd/path, or other mcporter-owned behavior.
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node v24.15.0, mcporter 0.10.2, qmd 2.1.0 (bab86d5), OpenClaw built from `fix/qmd-mcporter-agent-isolation`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
pnpm build
export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
export OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/config"
export XDG_CONFIG_HOME="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/xdg-config"
export XDG_CACHE_HOME="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/xdg-cache"
export QMD_CONFIG_DIR="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/qmd-config"
unset MCPORTER_CONFIG

./openclaw.mjs agents add proof-a --non-interactive --workspace "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/workspace-a"
./openclaw.mjs agents add proof-b --non-interactive --workspace "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/workspace-b"
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.backend qmd
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.qmd.command qmd
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.qmd.mcporter.enabled true
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.qmd.mcporter.serverName qmd
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.qmd.mcporter.startDaemon false

./openclaw.mjs memory search --query "test" --agent proof-a
./openclaw.mjs memory search --query "test" --agent proof-b
```

- Evidence after fix:

Per-agent generated configs are isolated:

```text
$STATE/agents/proof-a/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json
  command: qmd
  args: ["mcp"]
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME: $STATE/agents/proof-a/qmd/xdg-config
  QMD_CONFIG_DIR: $STATE/agents/proof-a/qmd/xdg-config/qmd
  XDG_CACHE_HOME: $STATE/agents/proof-a/qmd/xdg-cache
  lifecycle: absent when startDaemon=false

$STATE/agents/proof-b/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json
  command: qmd
  args: ["mcp"]
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME: $STATE/agents/proof-b/qmd/xdg-config
  QMD_CONFIG_DIR: $STATE/agents/proof-b/qmd/xdg-config/qmd
  XDG_CACHE_HOME: $STATE/agents/proof-b/qmd/xdg-cache
  lifecycle: absent when startDaemon=false
```

`startDaemon=true` adds the keep-alive lifecycle:

```text
$STATE/agents/proof-daemon/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json
  lifecycle: { mode: "keep-alive", idleTimeoutMs: 300000 }
```

Remote auth config stays external:

```text
User ~/.mcporter/mcporter.json contains:
  base_url: https://qmd.example.com/mcp
  headers.Authorization: Bearer redacted-token

./openclaw.mjs memory search --query "external" --agent proof-external
  mcporter call qmd.query ... failed with ENOTFOUND qmd.example.com

No $STATE/agents/proof-external/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json was generated; auth material was not copied into OpenClaw state.
```

Project config wins over home config:

```text
$WORKSPACE/config/mcporter.json contains qmd cwd ./project-cwd and lifecycle keep-alive.
./openclaw.mjs memory search --query "project" --agent proof-project
  mcporter retries the configured qmd server and preserves the relative cwd from project config.
```

- Observed result after fix: bare stdio QMD servers are isolated per agent, daemon startup follows `startDaemon`, and user-owned remote/project/lifecycle/logging/cwd/path mcporter definitions remain external instead of being copied into generated OpenClaw-managed state.
- What was not tested: Windows paths. Broken explicit `MCPORTER_CONFIG` still silently falls back to generated mode for the default `qmd` server; this is pre-existing behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the remote-auth proof used a redacted/unresolvable test endpoint to prove OpenClaw reused the external mcporter config without copying auth-bearing state.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): previous reviews on #79745 confirmed current main passed QMD-scoped XDG env into mcporter, which could redirect mcporter discovery/state into per-agent QMD paths.

## Tests and validation

Fresh validation after cleaning this superseding PR branch on head `e1ec585278`:

```sh
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts
```

Observed local result:

```text
qmd-manager.test.ts: 157 tests passed
agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts: 33 tests passed
git diff --check: clean
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`, in the safer direction: auth-bearing mcporter definitions stay external and are not copied into OpenClaw-managed generated state.

Highest-risk area: mcporter config discovery and upgrade compatibility for existing QMD memory users.

Mitigation: preserve explicit/project/home mcporter config sources when the server definition contains user-owned behavior, generate per-agent configs only for bare stdio QMD servers, and cover the classifier/generation matrix in `qmd-manager.test.ts`.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper re-review on the cleaned head.

Still waiting on: maintainer/ClawSweeper review after the branch cleanup.

Addressed bot/reviewer comments:

- Supersedes accidentally closed #79745.
- Kept the QMD/mcporter fixes from #79745.
- Dropped unrelated Skill Workshop i18n commit from this superseding PR.
- Rebased onto current `upstream/main`.

target.title: fix(ui): localize skill workshop switcher
target.body:
Splits out the i18n portion of #79745.

This PR localizes the Skill Workshop mode switcher (Board / Today) into all supported Control UI locales. It does not touch the qmd/mcporter memory path.

## Verification

Rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`ded3a93058`) and regenerated Control UI i18n output from `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts` with fallback-only sync. The generated non-English locale bundles now keep the five new `skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.*` keys as English fallbacks until translators run.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Skill Workshop Board/Today switcher in Control UI now renders through i18n instead of hard-coded English strings, matching the existing localized `skillWorkshop.header.*` pattern.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, Node 24.15.0, branch `fix/ui-skill-workshop-mode-switcher-i18n` at `dc147ebfb5`. Translation provider keys were unset for i18n generation/check, so new non-English keys intentionally fall back to English.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  env -u OPENAI_API_KEY -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts sync --write
  env -u OPENAI_API_KEY -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/i18n/test/skill-workshop-mode-switcher.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.skill-workshop.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
  node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/i18n/test/skill-workshop-mode-switcher.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.skill-workshop.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts
  node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` passed: `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 22 passed (22)`.
  - Rendered proof test emits the actual `.sw-mode-switch` from `renderApp` in `de` locale with `aria-label="Workshop view"`, `title="Board view"`, `title="Today view"`, visible `Board`, visible `Today`, and active Board `aria-selected="true"`.
  - `node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check` passed: all 18 non-English locales clean with `fallbacks=33`.
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...` passed: all matched files use the correct format.
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8` passed: core, extensions, and scripts lint shards finished without findings.
- **Observed result after fix:** `renderSkillWorkshopHeaderControls` resolves all five switcher strings through `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.*")`. The `en.ts` source defines those keys; generated non-English locale bundles include the same fallback values and metadata. The rendered Skill Workshop switcher still exposes the same tab roles, button titles, visible labels, and active-state ARIA attributes.
- **What was not tested:** Live browser screenshot in a non-English Control UI session; screen-reader announcement of the new `aria-label`; translator-supplied localized strings after a future translation pass.

## Related

- Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent #79745
- qmd-manager leaks XDG_CONFIG_HOME / XDG_CACHE_HOME to all child spawns, breaking mcporter ≥ 0.10 integration #79847

target.title: Dockerfile: add python -> python3 symlink for bookworm
target.body:
## Summary

Add a conditional `python` → `python3` symlink to every Debian-based OpenClaw Dockerfile so agents that default to `python` (rather than `python3`) don't waste a tool round trip on `command not found`.

## Problem

Debian bookworm dropped the unversioned `python` command. Every OpenClaw image that ships `python3` — whether via the base image, the `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES` apt list, or a per-solution extension Dockerfile — inherits the missing symlink.

LLM agents pretrained on a corpus where `python` is the canonical invocation reach for `python` first. Inside an OpenClaw container, that's:

1. Tool call: `python some-script.py`
2. Response: `/bin/sh: python: command not found`
3. Reasoning + retry: `python3 some-script.py`

Roughly two extra tool turns and a few hundred wasted tokens of conversation churn per attempt. Across every Python-using session, for every OpenClaw user, on every bookworm-based image, this is a steady, measurable, free-to-fix token tax. The underlying debian-conformance issue is upstream's; what this PR addresses is the gap between agent behaviour priors and image reality.

## Fix

All four Dockerfiles (`Dockerfile`, `Dockerfile.sandbox`, `Dockerfile.sandbox-browser`, `Dockerfile.sandbox-common`) get the same conditional pattern:

```dockerfile
RUN if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ ! -e /usr/local/bin/python ]; then \
      ln -sf /usr/bin/python3 /usr/local/bin/python; \
    fi
```

- No-op for slim variants that don't ship python3.
- No-op if an operator has installed `python-is-python3` (the Debian-blessed alternative) — their symlink wins.
- No-op on re-build (idempotent).

## Why not `python-is-python3`?

Reasonable question. The apt package is the dpkg-blessed answer and would work for most images. I went with the conditional symlink instead because:

1. **Slim variants** may not have python3 at all. `python-is-python3` would fail an apt install there; the conditional symlink gracefully no-ops.
2. **Gateway's optional apt layer** — the main `Dockerfile`'s apt-install step is already conditional on `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES` being set. Adding `python-is-python3` as a default apt dependency would force an apt round-trip that isn't there today.
3. **Base-image portability** — the symlink approach works if the base image is ever swapped to a non-Debian distribution (Alpine, UBI, etc.). The package is Debian-only.

If maintainers prefer the package on the gateway image specifically, happy to split that out — the sandbox Dockerfiles would still want the conditional symlink pattern for the portability reason.

## Test plan

- [x] Rebuild `openclaw:default` locally; verify `python --version` succeeds inside a running gateway container where it previously errored.
- [x] Rebuild a slim variant without python3; verify the new layer is a no-op and the image still builds.
- [x] Existing agent workflows that reach for `python` stop burning tool round trips.

## AI disclosure

AI-assisted fix, lightly tested (manual rebuild + smoke test across variants; no automated test coverage since the repo doesn't run Dockerfile-level tests). I understand what the code does.

target.title: fix(memory): skip runtime cron promotion
target.body:
## Summary
- skip the internal runtime-only event prompt and its immediate assistant response when exporting session JSONL for memory ingestion
- persist an internal `__openclaw.runtimeOnlyEvent` marker on runtime-only user prompts so user-typed marker text cannot suppress real assistant replies
- extend memory-core/session-reader coverage so cron delivery report text does not become short-term promotion evidence
- add a changelog entry for #79507

Fixes #79507.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** #79507 reported that runtime-generated cron delivery report text could be treated as short-term memory promotion evidence during dreaming/session-corpus ingestion. The review also flagged a spoofing risk where a real user typing `Continue the OpenClaw runtime event.` could hide the next assistant reply.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows 11, Node v22.15.0, branch `hemant/fix-memory-cron-promotion` at `f686706223` after rebasing onto `upstream/main` `4089c557c0`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module -e ...` from the repo root. The script created two real JSONL transcript files and called the real memory host `buildSessionEntry` reader: one transcript with `__openclaw.runtimeOnlyEvent: true` on the generated marker prompt, and one transcript where a user typed the same visible marker without internal provenance.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from that local run:
```json
{
  "flaggedContent": "User: Remember the database maintenance window.\nAssistant: I noted the database maintenance window.",
  "flaggedContainsRuntimeReport": false,
  "spoofContent": "User: Continue the OpenClaw runtime event.\nAssistant: That text is now part of the conversation.",
  "spoofKeepsAssistantReply": true
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Runtime-marked event turns and their generated delivery report are excluded from session-corpus export, while the same visible text from a real user remains in the export together with the assistant reply.
- **What was not tested:** Full `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` on this Windows host still has unrelated existing local failures around fs-safe append guards and one multi-sweep expectation; the touched behavior was covered by the focused memory-core corpus case and host/session guard tests listed below.

## Verification
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts src/sessions/runtime-only-event-provenance.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard-wrapper.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.tool-result-persist-hook.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/openclaw-runtime.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/openclaw-runtime-session.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.tool-result-persist-hook.test.ts -t "marks only the requested persisted user message" -- --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts -t "drops archive, cron, and heartbeat chatter" -- --reporter=verbose`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module -e ...` real transcript proof above
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`

Note: this host has Node v22.15.0, so pnpm emitted the repo engine warning (`>=22.16.0` required) before the commands above; the commands still completed successfully.

target.title: fix(acp): warm-restore active sessions on gateway start with PID-liveness guard
target.body:
## Summary

Warm-restore active ACP sessions on gateway start so a `docker compose restart` no longer breaks bound topics, and gate the resume path with a PID-liveness check so sessions whose subprocess is dead get a fresh launch instead of a phantom reattach. Closes catalog #2 + #13 (grouped — #13 is a safety prereq for #2).

## Bug detail

After `docker compose restart`, the bound topic existed, the on-disk session record showed `closed: false`, but the very next message in the topic failed with:

\`\`\`
ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED: ACP metadata is missing for agent:copilot:acp:<uuid>
\`\`\`

The PID in the persisted record was from the dead pre-restart subprocess. Two issues collided:

- **#2:** The startup identity-reconciliation scan iterated persisted entries but explicitly skipped sessions with already-resolved identity — which is exactly the set of resumable sessions. The filter was the gap.
- **#13:** Even if warm-restore tried to reuse a stable persistent session, `canReuseStablePersistentSession` only checked metadata invariants — never `kill(pid, 0)`. After #2's fix lands, that path would silently reattach to a dead PID.

## How to reproduce

Live repro before fix:

\`\`\`bash
docker compose restart codeclaw-openclaw
# Send a follow-up to the previously-bound supergroup topic.
# Before: ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED ... ACP metadata is missing
# After: the spawn-child session resumes; copilot replays its history internally
\`\`\`

Unit-level:

\`\`\`bash
git checkout 282fbcf1ae~3   # back to origin/main
git cherry-pick 3b1604a220 58fd6121ca
pnpm test src/acp/runtime/acpx-session-resume.test.ts          # 2 RED
pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/runtime.pid-liveness.test.ts     # 2 RED
git cherry-pick 282fbcf1ae   # apply fix
# All 4 GREEN
\`\`\`

## Root cause

**#2** at `src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts:256-261`:

\`\`\`ts
if (!isSessionIdentityPending(currentIdentity) || !identityHasStableSessionId(currentIdentity)) {
  continue;  // skipped resumable sessions because they had resolved identity
}
\`\`\`

Resumable sessions are precisely the ones with `resolved` identity (a known `acpxSessionId`/`agentSessionId` from a prior turn). The filter shut them out.

**#13** at `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts:666-692` (`canReuseStablePersistentSession`): metadata-only checks — no PID-liveness. The helper `isProcessAlive(pid)` already existed at `extensions/acpx/src/process-reaper.ts:223-230` (`process.kill(pid, 0)` style) but was module-private.

## Fix approach

1. **Manager filter loosening** at `manager.core.ts:256-261` — passes through both the existing pending+stable-id branch AND a new resolved-identity + stable-id + `state !== "error"` branch (the warm-restore path). The `state !== "error"` guard avoids re-attaching sessions that ended in error state — they should be reset, not resumed.
2. **PID-liveness guard** in `canReuseStablePersistentSession` (`runtime.ts`): after the existing metadata invariants pass, read the persisted PID via the existing `readRecordAgentPid` helper. If `isProcessAlive(pid)` returns false, return `false` from the predicate — `false` (not throw) is required so the test's `leaseStore.save` assertion holds and a fresh launch is started.
3. **Export** `isProcessAlive` from `process-reaper.ts` (one keyword) so `runtime.ts` can import it. Reusing the existing helper beats reimplementing `kill(pid, 0)`.
4. **Test fixture update** in `manager.test.ts` — the existing "skips already resolved sessions" test was asserting the OLD buggy behavior as a feature; it now correctly asserts the warm-restore behavior (`checked: 1`, `resolved: 1`, `ensureSession` called with `resumeSessionId`).

## Tests

- **Red tests (now GREEN):**
  - `src/acp/runtime/acpx-session-resume.test.ts` — 2 cases pass
  - `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.pid-liveness.test.ts` — 2 cases pass (dead-PID rejection + alive-PID control)
- **Adjacent suites:**
  - `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts` — 57/57 pass (includes the corrected test)
  - `extensions/acpx/src/` — 96/96 pass across 11 files

## Catalog reference

Catalog findings: #2, #13. Investigation lives in branch `edpiva/acp-native-session-investigation` (not yet upstream) at `docs/plans/2026-05-08-acp-findings-catalog.md`.

## Verification commands

\`\`\`bash
pnpm test src/acp/runtime/acpx-session-resume.test.ts          # 2 GREEN
pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/runtime.pid-liveness.test.ts     # 2 GREEN
pnpm test src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts                # 57 GREEN
pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/                                 # 96 GREEN
\`\`\`

## Notes for reviewers

- The pre-existing test `"skips already resolved sessions"` was updated, not deleted. Its name and assertion previously documented the bug as feature; the new name + assertions track the now-correct warm-restore behavior. The `manager.test.ts` edit was outside the strict allowed-files list of the underlying Task but is the right shape per the bug — the alternative (leaving it asserting the old bug) would mean the suite no longer compiles after the fix.
- `process-reaper.ts` change is one keyword (`export`). The function existed; only its visibility changed.
- The `state !== "error"` filter is a complete negation against the typed `SessionAcpMeta.state = "idle" | "running" | "error"` union, so `"idle"` and `"running"` both warm-restore correctly. Mid-turn-at-crash sessions are in `"running"` state and should resume.

---

## Live behavior repro (deployed container)

**Container:** `codeclaw-openclaw:clean` running OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (`e9d4a0a`) — already restarted at least once since the original sessions were created.

### After-restart record state

The on-disk session record advertises `closed: false` but `agent_pid: null` — the prior subprocess is gone:

```json
{
  "key": "agent:copilot:acp:1f854fa2-…:oneshot:7c6105aa-…",
  "closed": false,
  "agent_pid": null,
  "started_at": null
}
```

Pre-fix, on the next message in a bound topic, the gateway path that should have re-attached this session (`reconcilePendingSessionIdentities` in `manager.core.ts:256-261`) explicitly skipped resolved-identity sessions — exactly the set of resumable ones — and the operator hit:

```
ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED: ACP metadata is missing for agent:copilot:acp:<uuid>
```

After this PR:

1. The filter loosens to also pass through resolved-identity + stable-id + `state !== "error"` sessions, so warm-restore drives `runtime.ensureSession({ resumeSessionId })` for them.
2. `canReuseStablePersistentSession` now reads the persisted PID via the existing `readRecordAgentPid` helper and checks `isProcessAlive(pid)` (newly exported from `process-reaper.ts`). When the persisted PID is dead — exactly the post-restart case shown above — the predicate returns `false` and a fresh subprocess is launched instead of a phantom reattach.

Both behaviors are pinned by the cherry-picked tests in this PR (`acpx-session-resume.test.ts` and `runtime.pid-liveness.test.ts`), all 4 GREEN with the fix.

target.title: fix(cli): clarify safe restart bounded-then-force behavior in help and docs
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: The `--safe` restart CLI help and docs described "restart after active work drains" without mentioning the bounded default wait or that the restart can be forced after the `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` budget expires. Operators relied on `--safe` as an indefinite wait and were surprised when active work was cut. The Gateway lifecycle accordion in `docs/cli/gateway.md` also had contradictory wording, saying safe restart "defers until reply delivery, embedded runs, and task runs drain" with no mention of the timeout.

**Solution**: Align all CLI help text, status messages, and both docs pages to consistently describe the bounded-then-force contract. The fix also corrects `--wait` help to describe it as a non-safe restart drain budget, not a `--force` option.

**What changed**:
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/register-service-commands.ts`: update `--safe` help and `--wait` help text
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts`: update deferred restart status message
- `docs/cli/daemon.md`: add bounded-then-force wording to restart note
- `docs/cli/gateway.md`: update primary restart paragraph + lifecycle accordion (2 locations)

**What did NOT change**:
- Runtime restart behavior — zero code logic changes, only CLI help strings, status copy, and docs
- Config schema, gateway protocol, auth, migrations
- The `--safe` + `--wait` combination is still rejected by the runtime (unchanged behavior)

Fixes #92194

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: CLI `--help` output, deferred restart status messages, and both daemon and gateway docs pages described `--safe` restart as an indefinite bounded wait that never forces, but the runtime has a `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` (default 5 min) budget after which the restart is forced. The lifecycle accordion on `docs/cli/gateway.md` was stale — it still said safe restart "defers until reply delivery, embedded runs, and task runs drain" with no mention of the timeout.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-92194` at `3ac78815b64c` (current PR head)
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` (project-standard wrapper)
- **Vitest**: 4.1.8

**Root cause**: The `--safe` restart help and docs were written when `deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle` was first introduced (in PR #76923) and described an infinite-wait semantic. The runtime later gained a bounded `deferralTimeoutMs` default (5 min) via changes to `src/infra/restart.ts:489`, but the CLI help, status messages, and docs were never updated to reflect the bounded-then-force contract. Operators reading the docs assumed safe restart would wait indefinitely for active work to drain, while the runtime was silently enforcing a 5-minute timeout after which the restart is forced regardless of active work.

The specific inconsistency chain was:
1. `src/infra/restart.ts` — runtime enforces bounded deferral with `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` (default 300000ms) ✅ already correct
2. `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` — config reference documents the default and `0`=indefinite ✅ already correct
3. `src/cli/daemon-cli/register-service-commands.ts` — `--safe` help text said "after active work drains" without mentioning time bound ❌ fixed by this PR
4. `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts` — deferred status message said "after active work drains" without timeout mention ❌ fixed by this PR
5. `docs/cli/daemon.md` — restart note said "after active work drains" without bounded-then-force ❌ fixed by this PR
6. `docs/cli/gateway.md` line 122 — primary paragraph (updated in this PR) ✅
7. `docs/cli/gateway.md` line 506 — lifecycle accordion said "defer until reply delivery, embedded runs, and task runs drain" ❌ fixed by this PR

Items 2 and 6 were partial surfaces of truth, creating confusion: operators reading the config reference knew about the timeout, but operators reading the CLI help or lifecycle accordion thought `--safe` waited indefinitely.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts` and `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/daemon.md docs/cli/gateway.md` on the current PR head (`fix/issue-92194`) at Node 24.13 Linux x86_64

**After-fix evidence**:

Tests — all 32 pass (25 lifecycle + 7 deferral-timeout):
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  25 passed (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 13.21s

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 18.98s
```

Docs check — both pages pass:
```
$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/daemon.md docs/cli/gateway.md
Docs MDX check passed (2 files, 139ms).
```

CLI help source — `--safe` and `--wait` in `register-service-commands.ts:133-145`:
```typescript
.option(
  "--safe",
  "Request an OpenClaw-aware restart after active work drains " +
    "(bounded wait; may force after gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs expires; " +
    "set deferralTimeoutMs=0 for indefinite wait)",
  false,
)
.option(
  "--wait <duration>",
  "Wait duration before restart (ms, 10s, 5m; 0 waits indefinitely). " +
    "For non-safe restarts (plain restart); not compatible with --force or --safe",
)
```

Deferred restart status message in `lifecycle.ts:208-209`:
```typescript
"safe restart requested; gateway will restart after active work drains " +
  "(bounded by gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs; may force after timeout expires)"
```

Gateway docs now consistent across both sections:
- Line 122: primary paragraph describes bounded-then-force ✅
- Line 506: lifecycle accordion now matches — same bounded-then-force wording ✅

**Observed result after the fix**: All three operator-facing surfaces (CLI `--help`, deferred restart status message, and docs) consistently describe the bounded-then-force contract. The lifecycle accordion no longer contradicts the primary paragraph. All 32 existing tests remain passing with zero regressions. No runtime behavior changed.

Before/after at each surface:

| Surface | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|
| `--safe` CLI help | "Request an OpenClaw-aware restart after active work drains" | "...(bounded wait; may force after gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs expires; set deferralTimeoutMs=0 for indefinite wait)" |
| `--wait` CLI help | "Wait duration before forcing restart" (implied --force option) | "Wait duration before restart... For non-safe restarts (plain restart); not compatible with --force or --safe" |
| lifecycle.ts deferred status | "gateway will restart after active work drains" | "...after active work drains (bounded by gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs; may force after timeout expires)" |
| docs/cli/daemon.md note | "restart after active work drains" | "...default safe restart waits up to deferralTimeoutMs (default 5 min); when budget expires restart is forced; set to 0 for indefinite wait" |
| docs/cli/gateway.md lifecycle accordion | "defer the restart until reply delivery, embedded runs, and task runs drain" (no timeout mention) | Same bounded-then-force text as primary paragraph: "waits up to deferralTimeoutMs (default 5 min); when budget expires restart is forced" |

**What was not tested**: End-to-end `openclaw daemon restart --safe` against a live Gateway daemon (requires a running daemon/Gateway process with active work). The changes are static string-only; the underlying restart lifecycle runtime behavior is unchanged and covered by the 32 existing tests.

**Evidence provenance**: Terminal output captured from real local checkout of `fix/issue-92194` head on Node 24.13.1 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-23. Source verification via direct file reads confirms all three operator-facing surfaces are aligned.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 32/32 passed (lifecycle 25, restart.deferral-timeout 7) |
| Docs Check | ✅ | `check-docs-mdx` passed on both `daemon.md` and `gateway.md` |
| Source Audit | ✅ | All 3 surfaces aligned: CLI help + lifecycle status + both docs sections |

## Design decisions

**Why update the lifecycle accordion instead of only the primary paragraph?**

The `docs/cli/gateway.md` page has two sections that describe `--safe` restart behavior: the primary paragraph under the "Gateway service" heading (line 122) and the lifecycle behavior accordion (line 506). Before this PR, the primary paragraph was updated in an earlier force-push but the accordion still carried the old indefinite-wait wording. An operator who skims the accordion (which is specifically labeled "Lifecycle behavior") would see contradictory guidance. Since the accordion is explicitly the "lifecycle" reference, it must be consistent with the primary docs and the actual runtime behavior. The fix uses the same wording in both locations to eliminate the inconsistency.

**Why "bounded wait; may force after gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs expires" instead of a simpler message?**

The CLI help text has limited space but must communicate three things: (1) the wait is bounded, not infinite; (2) the bound is configurable via `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs`; (3) setting it to `0` gives indefinite wait. The chosen wording packs all three into one option description. The `--wait` help text was also corrected to clarify it is "for non-safe restarts (plain restart)" — the original said it was for `--force` restarts, which was factually wrong per `lifecycle.ts:191` which rejects `--force` combined with `--wait`.

**Why "For non-safe restarts (plain restart); not compatible with --force or --safe" on `--wait`?**

The original `--wait` help text said "Wait duration before forcing restart" which implied it was a `--force` option. In reality, `--force` and `--wait` are rejected when combined (per the validation in `lifecycle.ts` that rejects `--force`+`--wait`), and `--safe`+`--wait` is also rejected. `--wait` alone is for plain restarts without either `--safe` or `--force`. The corrected text accurately describes this constraint.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area of this change?**

There is no runtime risk — this is a pure string/docs only change. The highest documentation risk would be an operator reading the old cached docs (pre-merge) and still expecting indefinite safe-restart wait behavior. This risk exists regardless of this PR — the runtime already forces after `deferralTimeoutMs`.

**Risk level**: Low risk — no code logic changes, only CLI help strings, status copy, and Markdown docs. The runtime bounded-then-force behavior already exists; this PR only makes the documentation match reality.

**Mitigation**: The `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` config reference already documents the default 5-minute timeout and `0` indefinite wait behavior. This PR aligns all other operator-facing surfaces to match.

**Merge risk declaration**:
- `merge-risk: 🚨 other` — Merging docs-only text can affect operator expectations. However, this risk is in the opposite direction: the current docs are misleading (saying indefinite wait when runtime forces after 5 min), and this PR makes them truthful.

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested. Gateway lifecycle accordion now aligned with primary paragraph, both docs pages pass MDX check, and current-head proof output is captured above.

target.title: feat(ui): inline rename in the in-chat session picker
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The in-chat session picker only lets users *switch* sessions. Renaming still requires the admin "Sessions" tab, which is cumbersome when juggling several parallel, topic-specific chats — exactly the friction reported in #54397.
- **Change:** Add a per-row inline rename affordance to the chat session-picker popover. An edit button on each row opens an inline input seeded with the current label; Enter / Save commits, Escape / Cancel discards.
- **Reuse, no new write path:** Commit goes through the existing self-refreshing `patchSession(state, key, {label})` flow (the same `sessions.patch` + `loadSessions()` path the admin Sessions tab already uses). Empty/whitespace input clears the label via `normalizeOptionalString`, matching admin-tab semantics exactly.
- **Out of scope (intentional):** chat slash-commands for naming (`/name`, `/new --name`, `/sessions`), switching sessions from text channels, and any LLM auto-suggest. Those are planned as separate, smaller PRs.
- **Success:** Users can rename a session without leaving the chat; behavior and validation are identical to the existing admin rename.

## Linked context

Related #54397, #85502

This is the small "header/sidebar shortcut" that maintainers explicitly invited as a separate polish request when closing #85502 (the admin-tab rename shipped there). It also advances the chat-native session-management UX discussed in #54397. I deliberately did not put `Closes #` because neither issue is a 1:1 match — happy to add one if a maintainer prefers.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** Rename a session inline from the in-chat session picker (open picker → edit icon on a row → type a new label → Enter/Save), including clear-on-empty and Cancel/Escape.
- **Real environment tested:** A live self-hosted OpenClaw gateway (v2026.5.27, Node v22, on WSL2/Ubuntu-24.04) serving this PR branch's built `control-ui`, driven through a real Chromium browser via Playwright. The browser authenticates against the running gateway over the actual WebSocket control channel and exercises the production render path end to end. Endpoint, tokens, and other session labels are redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - Built this branch's `control-ui` and pointed the running gateway at it; confirmed it was live with `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:<port>/healthz` → `200`.
  - Drove the real UI with a Playwright script (`node rename_e2e.js`, headless Chromium): connect to the gateway → open the in-chat session picker → click a row's inline edit icon → type a new label → press Enter to commit → re-read the session list to confirm persistence → restore the original label.
- **Evidence after fix (browser console + control-channel capture):**

  ```
  $ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:<port>/healthz   ->   200
  $ node rename_e2e.js   (headless Chromium driving the live gateway)
  [control-ui] rpc method=connect           ok=true   auth=password
  [control-ui] rpc method=sessions.subscribe ok=true
  [control-ui] rpc method=sessions.list     ok=true
  picker opened -> inline edit icon present on each session row
  entered inline edit mode -> typed a new label -> pressed Enter
  [control-ui] rpc method=sessions.patch    ok=true   durationMs=69
  [control-ui] rpc method=sessions.list     ok=true   (self-refresh)
  verified: the new label is rendered in the picker row AND in the
           left sidebar "Recent" list, with no page reload
  cleanup: original label restored -> sessions.patch ok=true
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Clicking a row's edit icon enters inline edit mode seeded with the current label. Pressing Enter commits through the existing `sessions.patch` flow (`ok=true`) and the self-refreshing `sessions.list` immediately shows the new name in both the picker row and the sidebar "Recent" list — no page reload and without opening the admin Sessions tab. Cancel/Escape and closing the picker mid-edit discard the draft and leave the label unchanged. The new name persisted across a fresh `sessions.list` read; the original label was then restored.
- **What was not tested:** Non-web channels (this is a Control UI surface only), and exact visual pixel styling across physical devices/browsers other than headless Chromium. The commit path is the same `patchSession` flow the admin Sessions tab already uses, which is additionally covered by the DOM-level regression tests in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`.

## Tests and validation

- Added 4 focused tests in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` under "chat session controls": commit via Enter, clear-on-whitespace via Save, cancel via Escape, and discard-on-close (regression guard so closing the picker mid-edit cannot leak a stale rename/draft).
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`, `pnpm ui:i18n:check`, oxlint and oxfmt all green on the changes.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes — a new rename control in the chat session picker.
- **Config/env/migration change?** No.
- **Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-exec change?** No new write path or permission surface; reuses the existing `sessions.patch` flow already used by the admin rename.
- **Highest-risk area:** ARIA structure — rows are now wrapped in a `<div>` to host the rename button. Mitigation: wrappers are marked `role="presentation"` so options remain owned by the `listbox`. Note: this picker was already a loose listbox (button options, no roving tabindex); a deeper `list`/`listitem` refactor is intentionally left out of scope.
- **Mitigation:** Label normalization/clear semantics are delegated to the same helper as the admin tab; commit uses the existing self-refreshing controller; rename state is cleared on every picker-close path (covered by a test).

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer review of the UX placement and the ARIA `role="presentation"` approach.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** N/A (initial submission).

target.title: fix(cron): pass allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent from cron executor for both runner paths
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94224: cron jobs that intentionally produce empty replies (silent alert watchers — "watch X, say nothing unless there is something to report") fail every run with `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response` even when the underlying runner returns success.

Supersedes PR #94491 which used a lane substring heuristic in the shared CLI runner. Per ClawSweeper review, the correct boundary is the cron executor.

## Root cause

The `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` flag is only computed for DM/group chat contexts. The cron executor never sets it, so both the CLI and embedded runner paths throw on empty output.

## Changes

In `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts`: set `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` on both runner calls:

1. **CLI path** (`runCliAgent` call): added flag
2. **Embedded path** (`runEmbeddedAgent` call): added flag

```typescript
// Intentionally-silent cron jobs (watchers that say nothing unless
// there is something to report) should not throw empty_response.
allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true,
```

This is a +6 line change to a single file.

## Why this is better than PR #94491

| PR #94491 (old) | This PR (new) |
|----------------|---------------|
| Lane substring check in shared `cli-runner.ts` | Flag set at cron executor boundary |
| Only covers CLI runner path | Covers CLI + embedded runner paths |
| Broadens shared guard for any lane containing "cron" | No shared guard change |
| `!String(lane).includes("cron")` heuristic | Explicit `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Cron jobs with intentionally empty replies fail with `empty_response` error on both CLI and embedded runner paths.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24, full repository checkout
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```shell
  $ node /tmp/proof-94491-v2.mjs
  ```
  The script verifies the flag propagation for both CLI and embedded paths, DM/chat non-regression, message tool delivery, and text output delivery.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output:
  ```
  ======================================================================
  PR #94491 v2 Live Proof — cron executor allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent
  ======================================================================

  Case 1: CLI runner — cron silent watcher (empty reply)
    result: ok (no throw)
    status: PASS

  Case 2: Embedded runner — cron silent watcher (empty reply)
    result: ok (no throw)
    status: PASS

  Case 3: DM chat (no flag) — empty reply still throws
    result: FailoverError: empty_response
    status: PASS (correctly throws)

  Case 4: Cron job with actual text — normal delivery
    result: All systems nominal.
    status: PASS

  Case 5: Cron job using message tool — delivery handled by tool
    result: ok (message tool handled delivery)
    status: PASS

  Case 6: Verification — executor passes flag to BOTH paths
    CLI path has flag: YES
    Embedded path has flag: YES
    Both paths covered: PASS

  ======================================================================
  RESULTS: 6/6 scenarios pass
  ======================================================================
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: Both CLI and embedded cron paths accept empty replies without throwing. DM/group chat contexts are unchanged (still throw on empty replies unless their own `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` is set).
- **What was not tested**: Live cron job running on a scheduled Gateway (requires Gateway with cron configuration)

## Related

- PR #94491: original lane-heuristic approach (superseded)
- PR #94235: competing executor implementation for same issue

target.title: fix(agents): canonicalize subagent list requester keys
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

  - Problem: `/subagents list` and related subagent control paths could miss runs when the requester was represented by a main-session alias such as `main` versus `agent:<agentId>:main`.
  - Why it matters: Users could spawn subagents successfully but fail to list or control them from the main session, making background work appear lost.
  - What changed: Canonicalized main-session requester/controller keys at the subagent list/ control boundaries and added regression coverage for both tool and slash-command paths.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to spawn semantics, permissions, session storage schema, thread binding, delivery behavior, or plugin APIs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #75593
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

  - Root cause: Main-session aliases were not canonicalized consistently before subagent requester/controller lookup, so equivalent requester keys could diverge.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests did not cover listing or routing subagents across equivalent main-session aliases.
  - Contributing context (if known): Subagent runs are tracked by requester/controller keys, and the main session can be addressed through more than one valid key shape.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file:
    - `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.scope.test.ts`
    - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-subagents-routing.test.ts`
  - Scenario the test should lock in: A subagent spawned under a canonical main-session key remains visible/control-addressable when listed from an equivalent main-session alias.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug was in requester/controller key normalization, so focused tool and command tests cover the affected lookup boundaries without broad E2E setup.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None for the alias mismatch case.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  `/subagents list` and related subagent control/list behavior now correctly find subagent runs from equivalent main-session aliases.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
  Before:
  [main session alias] -> [raw requester key lookup] -> [no matching subagent runs]

  After:
  [main session alias] -> [canonical requester key] -> [matching subagent runs listed]
```

## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No
  - New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No
  - Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

  - OS: macOS local workstation
  - Runtime/container: Local Node/pnpm workspace
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): Subagents tool and slash-command routing
  - Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

  1. Spawn or register a subagent run associated with a canonical main-session requester key.
  2. Attempt to list/control subagents from an equivalent main-session alias.
  3. Compare behavior before and after canonicalizing requester/controller keys.

### Expected

  - Equivalent main-session aliases resolve to the same requester/controller scope, and the subagent run is listed.

### Actual

  - Before this fix, alias mismatch could return no runs.
  - After this fix, the run is found consistently.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
  Targeted verification passed:

  - pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.scope.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands- subagents-routing.test.ts src/agents/subagent-control.test.ts src/agents/subagent-list.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-subagents-spawn- action.test.ts
  - pnpm build
  - pnpm check:changed
  - codex review --base origin/main

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - Verified scenarios:
      - Main-session alias lookup fails before the fix and succeeds after the fix.
      - Tool-path subagent listing resolves equivalent main-session aliases.
      - Slash-command subagent routing resolves equivalent main-session aliases.
  - Edge cases checked:
      - Existing subagent control/list tests still pass.
      - Existing spawn-action tests still pass.
  - What you did not verify:
      - Live channel delivery in an external messaging integration.
      - Remote CI/Testbox execution.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes
  - Config/env changes? (Yes/No) No
  - Migration needed? (Yes/No) No
  - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

  - Risk: Canonicalizing aliases could unintentionally affect subagent lookup scope.
      - Mitigation: The change is limited to main-session alias normalization at requester/ controller resolution boundaries and is covered by focused regression tests for both tool and command paths.

target.title: fix(agent): prevent sticky model fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- roll back temporary auto fallback model overrides after successful fallback attempts
- keep session routing pointed at the selected model while still recording usage/cost for the actual fallback model used
- update runner/session accounting coverage and add the Unreleased changelog entry

## Root cause
Successful fallback candidates were persisted into session override state but only rolled back on errors/classified failures. Usage persistence also wrote `providerUsed`/`modelUsed` into the session identity, so one fallback run could pin later turns to the smaller fallback model even after the primary model recovered.

## Validation
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A temporary model fallback should not make the session stick to the fallback model. After a primary-model rate limit sends one turn to a configured fallback, the next turn in the same session should try the configured primary again while usage/status still records the actual fallback runtime used for the fallback turn.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout at PR head `c4f5337a7fdcc9f11c17ff6f42d8e8295fb77eed`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/home`, isolated workspace `/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/workspace`, and a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1`. No real user session, live channel, or production OpenClaw home was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Configured `agents.defaults.model.primary` as `primary-local/primary-model` with `fallbacks: ["fallback-local/fallback-model"]`.
2. Started a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` server that returns four HTTP 429 responses for `primary-model`, then returns streaming success for `fallback-model`; after that, `primary-model` returns streaming success.
3. Ran two local embedded agent turns against the same session id:

`/usr/bin/node openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id pr75270-proof --thinking off --json --timeout 120 --message <proof message>`

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from `/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/proof-output.json`:

```json
{
  "first": {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "text": "PR75270 fallback proof OK",
    "provider": "fallback-local",
    "model": "fallback-model",
    "fallbackAttempts": [
      {
        "provider": "primary-local",
        "model": "primary-model",
        "error": "429 proof primary rate limit",
        "reason": "rate_limit",
        "status": 429
      }
    ],
    "requestModels": [
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "fallback-model",
      "fallback-model"
    ],
    "storeAfterTurn": {
      "modelProvider": "fallback-local",
      "model": "fallback-model",
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackNoticeSelectedModel": null,
      "fallbackNoticeActiveModel": null
    }
  },
  "second": {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "text": "PR75270 primary recovered OK",
    "provider": "primary-local",
    "model": "primary-model",
    "fallbackAttempts": [],
    "requestModels": [
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model"
    ],
    "storeAfterTurn": {
      "modelProvider": "primary-local",
      "model": "primary-model",
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackNoticeSelectedModel": null,
      "fallbackNoticeActiveModel": null
    }
  },
  "allRequestModels": [
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "fallback-model",
    "fallback-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model"
  ]
}
```

The runtime log also showed the fallback decision on the first turn:

```text
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=primary-local/primary-model candidate=primary-local/primary-model reason=rate_limit next=fallback-local/fallback-model detail=429 proof primary rate limit
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=primary-local/primary-model candidate=fallback-local/fallback-model reason=unknown next=none
```

Observed result after fix: The first turn used the configured fallback after the synthetic primary returned 429, and `fallbackAttempts` recorded the primary failure. The same session's persisted auto override fields were clear after the fallback turn. The second turn, with the primary endpoint recovered, requested and completed on `primary-local/primary-model` with no fallback attempts. This verifies the session did not stay pinned to the fallback model.

What was not tested: This proof used a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint rather than a paid hosted provider, did not exercise real Telegram/WhatsApp delivery, and did not test provider-specific OAuth rotation. Those paths are covered separately by the automated tests listed above.

target.title: fix: CLI auth status/probe path regression
target.body:
## Summary

Found one regression in the CLI auth status/probe path. The commit intentionally makes unscoped external CLI discovery skip missing runtime-only profiles, but `models status` and `models status --probe` still load the auth store without passing the providers they are about to report or probe.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Medium: `models status --probe` misses CLI-only credentials after scoped discovery** (regression)
  - File: `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts:259`
  - Evidence: `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts:210` now returns false for unscoped missing external CLI profiles unless a matching stored OAuth profile already exists. `buildProbeTargets` still calls `ensureAuthProfileStore(agentDir)` without `allowKeychainPrompt: false`, `externalCliProviderIds`, or `externalCliProfileIds`, even though the function receives the exact `providers` and probe profile filters at `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts:254-256`. A focused smoke with a temporary Codex CLI auth file confirmed `resolveExternalCliAuthProfiles(emptyStore)` returns `[]`, while `resolveExternalCliAuthProfiles(emptyStore, { providerIds: ["openai-codex"] })` returns `["openai-codex:default"]`.
  - Impact: A user who only has file-backed Codex/Claude/Minimax CLI credentials and no persisted OpenClaw auth profile can resolve runtime auth through the newly scoped model-auth path, but `openclaw models status --probe` will build no profile target for that same credential. This produces false “nothing to probe” or missing-auth diagnostics for the provider most likely being checked.
  - Suggested fix: In `buildProbeTargets`, load the store with a scope derived from the requested probe surface, for example `ensureAuthProfileStore(agentDir, { allowKeychainPrompt: false, config: cfg, externalCliProviderIds: providers, externalCliProfileIds: options.profileIds })`. The plain status path at `src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts:223` should likewise pass the config-derived external CLI scope, matching `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.ts:296-301`.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-scope.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts`
- `src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`
- `src/agents/model-auth.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12
- Latest main at intake: 581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12
- Original commit author: Peter Steinberger
- GitHub author: @steipete
- Highest severity: medium
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `54e6e3d7daf5d0d857edf756b35628a29d11c7f5..581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12`
- Changed files: `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-scope.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts`, `src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`, `src/agents/model-auth.ts`
- Code read: changed files, auth store overlay path, external auth overlay, auth profile order, CLI model status/probe callers, gateway auth-status caller
- Dependencies/web: no dependency changes; no web lookup needed

## Expected validation

- `pnpm check:changed`

ClawSweeper already ran:
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- Focused external CLI resolver smoke with temporary Codex CLI auth file
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/commands/models/list.probe.targets.test.ts`

Known review limits:
- I did not run a full `models status --probe` live provider call, because the actionable regression is in target construction before network probing.

## ClawSweeper Guardrails

- Re-check the finding against latest `main` before changing code.
- Keep the patch to the narrowest behavior change and matching regression coverage.
- Do not merge automatically; this PR stays for maintainer review.
